task_completion.description: Open the properties or labeling dialog for the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for layer properties or labeling is open in gvSIG, and the title or context indicates it applies to the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer.

task_completion.description: Enable the labeling feature for the layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox such as 'Enable labeling', 'Show labels', or 'Draw labels' is checked within the layer's properties or labeling dialog.

task_completion.description: Select the 'NAME' field to be used for the labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown menu or text field for the label attribute/field clearly shows 'NAME' selected.

task_completion.description: Apply the labeling settings to the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The labeling dialog is closed or the 'Apply'/'OK' button is clicked, returning focus to the main map view.

task_completion.description: Country names are visible on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels such as 'Brazil', 'Russia', or 'Australia' are rendered over the corresponding country polygons in the main map view canvas.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the gvSIG graphical user interface to configure labels.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the gvSIG application window, menus, and dialog boxes being used, rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying project files directly.

integrity.description: The labels displayed on the map are genuinely rendered by gvSIG.
integrity.visual_evidence: The labels appear integrated into the map view canvas of gvSIG, matching standard GIS label rendering, and are not overlaid using an external image editor or text tool.

integrity.description: The agent modified the existing 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer rather than adding a fake layer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Table of Contents (layer list) on the left shows the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer is active, and no suspicious new layers with pre-rendered text were added to the project.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target layer is exactly named 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries'. The correct field to select for labeling is 'NAME'. When correctly applied, the map will display country names like 'Brazil', 'Russia', and 'Australia' over their respective polygons.
